page: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 21.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 1417 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and text-processing capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2367.0, alongside top-tier results in image processing and text processing. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 34.92 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.34 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is solid, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17385.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 41703.39. Memory bandwidth is measured at 2070.94 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 166.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-frequency Intel processor, this server is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and text processing. It demonstrates strong throughput for static web serving at 45.9 GB/sec and efficient small-model LLM inference, processing 2457.26 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. The high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation offer qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise databases and memory-bound applications that require high single-core clock speeds but do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a 24-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 504.0 GB of RAM, yielding 21.0 GB per core, and includes 1417 GB of local storage. The server lacks GPU resources and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2367.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core capabilities are supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and web serving workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and dense memory allocations without requiring GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 24 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and local storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor.
504.0 GB of RAM providing a high 21.0 GB memory-to-core ratio.
1417 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core performance and text processing capabilities.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and text processing.
No integrated GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
